Application: Use when an invoice contains an inactive or missing ABN; ABN does not match supplier records; GST is charged by a non-registered supplier; or GST is missing or incorrectly calculated
Responsibilities: Bill Processor rejects invoice with ABN/GST Issue; Supplier submits corrected invoice; Portal records actions and notifies stakeholders
Purpose: Ensure invoices comply with ATO ABN and GST requirements
Key Principles: Valid ABN required; GST only charged by registered suppliers; non-compliant invoices rejected before processing; actions documented in Portal
Acceptable Outcomes: Corrected invoice submitted; ABN or GST status updated
Remedy: Advise invoice rejected and client notified; provide guidance on correction
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to ABN/GST issue; corrected invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to supplier compliance issue.
Day 3: Supplier: Reminder to submit corrected invoice.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder; advised invoice will be rejected if unresolved.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when unit rates multiplied by quantities do not equal line totals; subtotal plus GST does not equal invoice total; or rounding errors create discrepancies
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance liaises with supplier; Supplier submits corrected invoice
Purpose: Prevent overpayment or underpayment due to mathematical errors
Key Principles: Invoices must be mathematically accurate; reject only after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Corrected invoice submitted
Remedy: Reject after Day 10 and notify client
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to calculation error; corrected invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed.
Day 3: Supplier: Reminder to submit corrected invoice.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder; advised invoice will be rejected if unresolved.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when client name is missing, generic, illegible, misspelled, or replaced with a reference code
Responsibilities: Bill Processor rejects invoice with Client Name Issue; Supplier submits corrected invoice
Purpose: Ensure invoices are attributable to the correct client
Key Principles: Client name mandatory; no payment without correct identification; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Corrected invoice submitted
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to missing or incorrect client name; corrected invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed.
Day 3: Supplier: Reminder to submit corrected invoice.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder; advised invoice will be rejected if unresolved.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when invoice does not show service date; units or hours; unit rate; or service type per line
Responsibilities: Bill Processor rejects invoice; Supplier submits fully itemised invoice
Purpose: Ensure transparency and auditability of services
Key Principles: Clear itemisation required; lump sums not accepted; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Itemised invoice submitted
Remedy: Advise rejection and provide itemisation guidance
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to insufficient itemisation; itemised invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed.
Day 3: Supplier: Reminder to submit itemised invoice.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder; advised invoice will be rejected if unresolved.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when invoice unit rate exceeds approved Support at Home price list without documented client consent
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance verifies pricing; Supplier provides acceptance or revised invoice
Purpose: Protect clients from unapproved pricing
Key Principles: Rates must not exceed approved limits; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Approved rate confirmed or invoice corrected
Remedy: Reject after Day 10 with Exceeds Master Price List
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to rate exceeding approved price list; evidence of client consent or revised invoice requested.Client: Notified pricing review required.
Day 3: Supplier: Follow-up requesting consent evidence or corrected invoice.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder; advised invoice will be rejected if unresolved.Client: Advised rejection pending if no resolution.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when service dates fall outside the active quarterly funding period
Responsibilities: Bill Processor verifies service date and quarter; Compliance confirms eligibility; Supplier amends invoice or confirms private billing
Purpose: Ensure services are billed within correct funding quarter
Key Principles: Quarter dates apply strictly; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Invoice corrected to eligible quarter; private billing confirmed
Remedy: Advise rejection under Quarter End Date Issue and explain quarter rules
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to service date outside current quarter.Client: Notified invoice under review due to quarter timing.
Day 7: Supplier: Final clarification request if service date correction possible.Client: Advised outcome pending.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if outside quarter.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when service dates fall outside approved funding start or end dates or funding is exhausted
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance verifies funding status; Supplier amends invoice or confirms private billing
Purpose: Prevent payment for services outside eligible funding period
Key Principles: Funding dates must be respected; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Invoice corrected; client elects private payment
Remedy: Advise rejection under Funding Period Issue
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to service date outside approved funding period; amendment or confirmation of private billing requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to funding eligibility issue.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if service date remains ineligible.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when supplier registration is incomplete due to missing documents
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance checks outstanding requirements; Supplier completes registration
Purpose: Ensure suppliers are verified before payment
Key Principles: Supplier registration mandatory; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Supplier completes registration and resubmits invoice
Remedy: Advise rejection due to incomplete registration
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to incomplete supplier registration; outstanding documentation requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to supplier registration status.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder to complete registration requirements.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if registration not completed.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when no registered supplier record is found for the provided ABN
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance attempts match; Supplier registers
Purpose: Prevent payment to unverified suppliers
Key Principles: Supplier must be registered; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Supplier registers and resubmits invoice
Remedy: Reject invoice and notify client and supplier
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to no registered supplier record; registration requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to supplier not registered.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder to register to enable payment.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if registration not completed.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Reason: Supplier not registered for service type
Application: Use when supplier invoices for a service type they are not approved to provide
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance validates service registration; Supplier updates registration or amends invoice
Purpose: Ensure services are delivered by authorised suppliers
Key Principles: Correct service registration required; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Supplier registration updated or invoice corrected
Remedy: Reject under Supplier Not Registered for Service Type
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to supplier not approved for billed service type; registration update or revised invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to supplier service eligibility issue.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder to update service registration or amend invoice.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if service type not approved.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when supplier is under active performance management or PIP
Responsibilities: Accounts or Care Teams place invoice on hold; Compliance manages performance process
Purpose: Protect client safety and service quality
Key Principles: Client safety priority; evidence-based escalation; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Performance issue resolved or supplier restricted
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to active performance improvement or suspension.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to supplier performance review.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if supplier remains suspended.Client: Notified invoice rejected or service transition required.
Extra Cadence: Duration: No fixed timeframe; hold and review period determined by Compliance / Quality teams based on risk assessment.

Application: Use when invoice includes a rate higher than approved pricing without prior approval
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold; Compliance verifies pricing; Care Partner confirms client consent
Purpose: Prevent unapproved price increases
Key Principles: Client approval required; reject after 10 days unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Client approves rate or invoice corrected
Remedy: Reject under Price Increase – Not Approved
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to unapproved price increase; evidence of client consent or revised invoice requested.Client: Notified price approval required.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder to provide consent evidence or revised invoice.Client: Final reminder to confirm price approval.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if no approval received.Client: Notified invoice rejected.
Extra Cadence: Duration: Additional time may be allowed only where client engagement is active; no defined extension period specified.

Application: Use when invoice is submitted for services dated after supplier termination date
Responsibilities: Bill Processor rejects invoice; Compliance confirms termination; Care Team supports client transition
Purpose: Prevent payment to terminated suppliers
Key Principles: No payment after termination date; immediate rejection if post-termination; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Invoice corrected for eligible dates only; client transitions supplier
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice cannot proceed due to supplier termination status.Client: Notified invoice delayed or cannot proceed due to supplier termination.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if service date is after termination date.Client: Notified invoice rejected and advised on alternative provider arrangements if required.

Application: Use when multiple service types across contribution categories are bundled together
Responsibilities: Bill Processor rejects invoice; Supplier resubmits itemised invoice
Purpose: Ensure correct contribution category charging
Key Principles: Itemisation mandatory across contribution categories; no interpretation of bundled charges
Acceptable Outcomes: Fully itemised invoice submitted
Remedy: Reject under Services Not Itemised Across Contribution Categories
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice rejected due to services bundled across contribution categories; itemised invoice required.Client: Notified invoice rejected due to invoicing non-compliance.

Application: Use when transport charges are unclear, bundled, or incorrectly billed
Responsibilities: Bill Processor places invoice on hold or rejects; Supplier corrects transport itemisation
Purpose: Prevent incorrect transport charging
Key Principles: Transport must be itemised; staff travel not claimable; reject after 10 days unresolved
Acceptable Outcomes: Corrected invoice submitted
Remedy: Reject under Transport Not Itemised or Incorrect
Day 0: Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to incorrect or unclear transport charging; corrected invoice requested.Client: Notified invoice delayed due to transport billing issue.
Day 7: Supplier: Final reminder to submit corrected invoice.
Day 10: Supplier: Invoice rejected if transport remains non-compliant.Client: Notified invoice rejected.

Application: Use when insufficient funds exist in the client Home Care Package and no prior arrangement is in place
Responsibilities: Accounts places invoice on hold; Care Partner and Coordinator liaise with client; Finance processes voluntary contributions
Purpose: Prevent payment when package funds are insufficient
Key Principles: Client given at least 7 days to respond; reject after Day 10; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Budget updated or voluntary contribution made
Remedy: Advise rejection due to insufficient funds
Day 0: Client: Notified invoice on hold due to insufficient funds; response required within 7 days.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Notified invoice on hold pending client response.
Day 3: Client: Reminder to make arrangements.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 7: Client: Final notice to make arrangements to avoid rejection.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 10: Client: Invoice rejected due to insufficient funds.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Invoice rejected; advised to contact client directly.
Extra Cadence: Day 8: 48-hour extension granted to client to make arrangements before rejection.

Application: Use when invoice is for an in-scope service not included in the approved client budget
Responsibilities: Accounts places invoice on hold; Care Partner confirms service intent; Client confirms approval
Purpose: Ensure services are funded only if budget-approved
Key Principles: Client given at least 7 days to respond; reject after Day 10; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Budget updated or client elects private payment
Remedy: Advise rejection and offer budget review
Day 0: Client: Notified service not included in approved budget; confirmation required within 7 days.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Notified invoice on hold pending client instruction.
Day 3: Client: Reminder to confirm service intent.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 7: Client: Final notice to confirm budget update or private payment.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 10: Client: Invoice rejected due to no response.Coordinator: Notified.Supplier: Invoice rejected; advised to contact client directly.
Extra Cadence: Day 8: 48-hour extension granted to update care plan and budget before rejection.

Application: Use when invoice cannot proceed due to an exception requiring coordinator approval
Responsibilities: Accounts places invoice on hold; Care Partner liaises; Coordinator approves or rejects
Purpose: Ensure oversight for care or budget exceptions
Key Principles: Coordinator contacted first; reject after Day 10 unresolved; actions documented
Acceptable Outcomes: Approval granted or invoice rejected
Remedy: Advise rejection due to no approval received
Day 0: Coordinator: Notified approval required for invoice to proceed.Client: Notified invoice on hold pending coordinator review.Supplier: Notified invoice on hold awaiting approval.
Day 3: Coordinator: Reminder approval still required.Client: Notified invoice remains on hold.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 7: Coordinator: Final reminder; advised invoice may be rejected if no response.Client: Notified next steps pending.Supplier: Update provided.
Day 10: Coordinator: Invoice rejected due to no response.Client: Notified invoice rejected.Supplier: Invoice rejected; advised to follow up directly.
Extra Cadence: Day 8: Client given 48 hours to approve invoice directly before rejection.

Application: Use when the client’s available package funds are insufficient to cover the invoiced service amount
Responsibilities: System: Auto communications issued on hold.Care Partner: Review quarterly budget allocation and current spend; check unspent funds; confirm service is approved in the client support plan; assess funding availability; communicate funding position to client and supplier; where funding is insufficient, communicate requirement for voluntary contribution to cover invoice amount; review client needs and alignment with available budget; consider whether a package level review is required; determine whether voluntary contribution is feasible.Billing: Reject invoice where sufficient funds or voluntary contribution cannot be confirmed.
Purpose: Ensure invoices are not paid when package funding is insufficient while providing clients an opportunity to resolve funding gaps appropriately
Key Principles: Funding availability must be confirmed before payment; services must align with the approved support plan; voluntary contributions may be used where appropriate; invoices cannot proceed without confirmed funding
Acceptable Outcomes: Funding availability confirmed; voluntary contribution agreed and documented; package review initiated; invoice rejected where funding cannot be resolved
Day 0: Client: Email noting bill cannot be paid due to insufficient balance; request VC or CP contact within 48 hours.
Day 3: Client: Reminder to contact VC or CP.
Day 7: Client: Reminder advising bill will be rejected if no response within 48 hours.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to insufficient balance.
Day 10: Client: Bill rejected notification.Supplier: Bill rejected notification.

Application: Use when a record is flagged for exception and client approval is required before payment can proceed
Responsibilities: System: Auto communications issued advising approval required.Care Partner: Review exception details; confirm reason client approval is required; contact client to request approval; record client response; where approval is provided, confirm and progress record; where approval is not provided or no response is received, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Ensure client consent is obtained before payment is made
Key Principles: Client approval is mandatory where flagged; payment cannot proceed without explicit approval; decisions must be recorded
Acceptable Outcomes: Client approval confirmed and recorded; invoice progressed or rejected
Day 0: Client: Approval request sent.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold pending client approval.
Day 3: Client: Reminder approval required.Supplier: Update invoice remains on hold.
Day 7: Client: Follow-up advising bill will be rejected if approval not received.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to no client approval.
Day 10: Client: Bill rejected notification.Supplier: Bill rejected notification.

Application: Use when a record is flagged for exception and coordinator approval is required before payment can proceed
Responsibilities: System: Auto communications issued advising approval required.Care Partner: Review exception details; request coordinator approval; record coordinator response; where approval is provided, confirm and progress record; where approval is not provided or no response is received, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Ensure appropriate care oversight before payment
Key Principles: Coordinator approval is mandatory where flagged; payment cannot proceed without approval; actions must be recorded
Acceptable Outcomes: Coordinator approval confirmed and recorded; invoice progressed or rejected
Day 0: Coordinator: Approval request sent.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold pending coordinator approval.
Day 7: Coordinator: Follow-up advising bill will be rejected if approval not received.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to no coordinator approval.

Application: Use when unplanned services have reached the approved funding cap
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review current spend against cap; assess whether services can continue within existing cap; review need for budget update; discuss options with client; where cap increase is approved, update budget; where no approval or funding is available, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Prevent spending beyond approved caps
Key Principles: Spending must not exceed approved caps; budget updates are required for continuation
Acceptable Outcomes: Budget updated and invoice progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to confirm whether service should be added to budget.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to cap reached.
Day 7: Client: Advised bill will be rejected if budget update not approved.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to cap not increased.

Application: Use when the invoiced service relates to a funding stream that has not been verified
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Identify unverified funding stream; review funding eligibility; confirm correct funding source; where funding is verified, update record; where funding cannot be verified, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Ensure only verified funding streams are used
Key Principles: Funding streams must be verified before payment
Acceptable Outcomes: Funding verified and invoice progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to confirm funding stream or budget update.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to unverified funding.
Day 7: Client: Advised bill will be rejected if funding not verified.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to unverified funding.

Application: Use when the invoiced service is not included in the approved care plan or NOD
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review invoiced service against approved care plan or NOD; confirm whether service can be approved; where approval is granted, update plan; where service is not approved, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Prevent payment for services outside approved scope
Key Principles: Only approved services may be funded
Acceptable Outcomes: Service approved and invoice progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to confirm whether service should be approved/added to plan.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to unapproved service.
Day 7: Client: Advised bill will be rejected if service not approved.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to unapproved service.

Application: Use when the invoice relates to Assistive Technology and Home Modifications
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Identify ATHM invoice; remove from automated billing flow; manage assessment, approval, and processing manually in line with ATHM requirements.
Purpose: Ensure ATHM items follow correct assessment and approval pathways
Key Principles: ATHM items are not processed via standard billing automation
Acceptable Outcomes: Item assessed and processed manually

Application: Use when the invoiced rate exceeds the approved budgeted hourly rate
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review invoiced rate against approved rate; assess whether rate increase can be approved; discuss options with client; where rate is approved, update budget; where rate is not approved, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Prevent payment above approved rates
Key Principles: Rates must align with approved budget
Acceptable Outcomes: Rate approved and invoice progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to confirm whether rate increase should be approved.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to rate exceeding budget.
Day 7: Client: Advised bill will be rejected if rate not approved.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to rate not approved.

Application: Use when the invoiced quantity exceeds the approved service quantity
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review invoiced quantity against approved quantity; assess whether additional quantity can be approved; discuss options with client; where quantity is approved, update budget; where quantity is not approved, progress to rejection and notify supplier.
Purpose: Prevent payment beyond approved quantities
Key Principles: Quantities must align with approved plan
Acceptable Outcomes: Quantity approved and invoice progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to confirm whether additional quantity should be approved.Supplier: Invoice placed on hold due to quantity exceeded.
Day 7: Client: Advised bill will be rejected if quantity not approved.Supplier: Advised bill pending rejection due to quantity not approved.

Application: Use when the service date is before the client’s commencement date
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review service date; confirm with client whether service was provided; determine eligibility; where service is confirmed and eligible, progress; where not eligible, reject invoice and notify supplier.
Purpose: Ensure services are billed within eligible dates
Key Principles: Services must fall within valid commencement period
Acceptable Outcomes: Service confirmed and progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Supplier: Rejection letter sent outlining service date, active period, and reason service is outside commencement date; advised to pursue payment directly with client if applicable.

Application: Use when the service date is after the client’s exit or termination date
Responsibilities: Care Partner: Review service date; confirm with client whether service was provided; determine eligibility; where service is confirmed and eligible, progress; where not eligible, reject invoice and notify supplier.
Purpose: Ensure services are billed within active care period
Key Principles: Services must fall within approved care period
Acceptable Outcomes: Service confirmed and progressed; or invoice rejected
Day 0: Supplier: Rejection letter sent outlining service date, active period, and reason service is outside termination date; advised to pursue payment directly with client if applicable.

Application: Use when the client record in MYOB does not contain valid bank details and payment cannot be processed
Responsibilities: Accounts: Identify missing or invalid client bank details; attempt to obtain correct bank details; record outcome; where bank details cannot be obtained, progress to rejection
Purpose: Ensure payments are only made to verified bank accounts
Key Principles: Valid bank details are required before payment
Acceptable Outcomes: Bank details obtained and payment proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to request missing or corrected bank details.
Day 10: Client: Notified bill rejected due to missing bank details.Supplier: Notified bill rejected.

Application: Use when the supplier record in MYOB does not contain valid bank details and payment cannot be processed
Responsibilities: Accounts: Identify missing or invalid supplier bank details; attempt to obtain correct bank details from supplier; record outcome; where bank details cannot be obtained, progress to rejection
Purpose: Ensure payments are only made to verified supplier accounts
Key Principles: Valid supplier bank details are required before payment
Acceptable Outcomes: Bank details obtained and payment proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 0: Supplier: Contacted to request missing or corrected bank details.
Day 10: Supplier: Notified bill rejected due to missing bank details.Client: Notified bill rejected.

Application: Use when a reimbursement request is submitted without proof of payment
Responsibilities: Accounts: Identify missing proof of payment; request proof from client; record response; where proof cannot be obtained, progress to rejection
Purpose: Ensure reimbursements are supported by evidence of payment
Key Principles: Proof of payment is required for reimbursement
Acceptable Outcomes: Proof obtained and reimbursement proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to request proof of payment.
Day 10: Client: Notified bill rejected due to no proof of payment.

Application: Use when a reimbursement request is submitted without the original supplier invoice
Responsibilities: Accounts: Identify missing source invoice; request original invoice from client; record response; where invoice cannot be obtained, progress to rejection
Purpose: Ensure reimbursements are supported by valid invoices
Key Principles: Original supplier invoice is mandatory
Acceptable Outcomes: Invoice obtained and reimbursement proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 0: Client: Contacted to request original supplier invoice.
Day 10: Client: Notified bill rejected due to missing source invoice.

Application: Use when a bill fails final Finance QA review
Responsibilities: Finance: Identify QA failure; document issue; where QA failure cannot be resolved, reject bill
Purpose: Prevent payment of bills that fail finance quality assurance
Key Principles: Bills must pass Finance QA before payment
Acceptable Outcomes: QA issue resolved and bill proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 10: Client: Notified bill rejected following Finance QA failure.Supplier: Notified bill rejected.

Application: Use when the issue does not fit into another defined accounts reason and requires review
Responsibilities: Accounts: Review issue; determine appropriate resolution path; communicate with supplier if required; where issue cannot be resolved, progress to rejection
Purpose: Ensure undefined issues are reviewed and resolved consistently
Key Principles: All bills must meet minimum processing requirements
Acceptable Outcomes: Issue resolved and bill proceeds; or bill rejected
Day 0: Supplier: Contacted regarding issue requiring clarification.
Day 3: Supplier: Follow-up reminder sent if clarification not received.
Day 10: Supplier: Notified bill rejected if unresolved.
